channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How 2025’s digital products will evolve
item.description: When most people hear “TikTok features,” there’s usually some eye-rolling involved. But after 17 years of helping companies navigate digital transformation, I’ve noticed something more profound happening.
item.dc:creator: Brad Weber, Fast Company

item.title: The EU makes an urgent TikTok inquiry on Russia's role in Romanian election turmoil
item.description: The European Union has sent TikTok an urgent request for more information about Romanian intelligence files suggesting that Moscow coordinated influencers on its platform to promote an election candidate who became the surprise front-runner in the nation’s presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ally Financial finds gen AI success with 3 guiding principles
item.description: Bank holding company Ally Financial is determined to stay at the cutting edge of technology in the financial industry. Today, that means leveraging gen AI to transform its business.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Future of SAP Architectures: A Hazy Outlook for Many Organizations
item.description: SAP architectures are shifting towards hybrid, but many organizations remain uncertain about their future plans, with nearly half still using on-prem ERP Central Component (ECC) and 24% seeking to migrate to S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Public cloud providers are fumbling the AI opportunity
item.description: Generative AI is exploding and will continue to do so. As such, both on-premises and public cloud providers have seen a boom in demand for their AI wares, which will likely continue for at least the next five years. Cloud is, of course, a core benefit of companies’ intererest in AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: China's ban on key high-tech materials could have broad impact on industries, economy
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has banned exports of key materials used to make a wide range of products, including smartphones, electric vehicles, radar systems and CT scanners, swiping back at Washington after it expanded export controls to include dozens of Chinese companies that make equipment used to pro
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Michigan Democrats Push for Reproductive Health Legislation Amid Republican Takeover
item.description: Michigan lawmakers aim to pass digital health data protection bill ahead of GOP control in 2025
item.dc:creator: Isabella Volmert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: These satellites are on a mission to create the first fake solar eclipses. Here’s how
item.description: A pair of European satellites rocketed into orbit Thursday on the first mission to create artificial solar eclipses through fancy formation flying in space. Each fake eclipse should last six hours once operations begin next year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI unveils API for tracking OpenAI API usage, costs
item.description: OpenAI has introduced the Usage API, an API that allows enterprises to track their activity across the OpenAI API. With the Usage API, OpenAI API usage and costs can be assessed programmatically, the company said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The White House reveals at least 8 U.S. telecom firms impacted by China’s Salt Typhoon cyberattack
item.description: A top White House official on Wednesday said at least eight U.S. telecom firms and dozens of nations have been impacted by a Chinese hacking campaign.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: The power of unified data storage with generative AI
item.description: The promise of generative AI (genAI) is undeniable, but the volume and complexity of the data involved pose significant challenges.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: BERKELEY APPROVES ZONING CHANGES TO ATTRACT RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT COMPANIES
item.description: Berkeley, CA - The city council votes to permit labs studying biosafety levels 2 in mixed-use light industrial zoning areas while prohibiting level 3 and 4 uses
item.dc:creator: Sierra Lopez, The Mercury News

item.title: As data centers proliferate, conflict with local communities follows
item.description: Some residents are pushing back against the world’s most powerful corporations over concerns about the economic, social and environmental health of their communities.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple shops at Amazon for Apple Intelligence services
item.description: Apple shops at Amazon. In this case, it is using artificial intelligence (AI) processors from Amazon Web Services (AWS) for some of its Apple Intelligence and other services, including Maps, Apps, and search.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: From outsider to the Oval Office, bitcoin surges as a new administration embraces crypto
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Born from the ashes of the 2008 global financial crisis, bitcoin arrived after trust had withered in the financial system and in Washington's ability to protect those who must participate in it.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, PIX 11
